file.content:
## Flotsam API Makefile
## ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Usage: make <target>
## Targets requiring DATABASE_URL: migrate-up, migrate-down, migrate-status, migrate-create

MODULE      := github.com/ketang/flotsam/api
VERSION     := $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev)
BUILD_TIME  := $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
LDFLAGS     := -ldflags "-X $(MODULE)/internal/version.Version=$(VERSION) \
                          -X $(MODULE)/internal/version.BuildTime=$(BUILD_TIME)"

GOLANGCI_LINT := $(shell which golangci-lint 2>/dev/null || echo $(shell go env GOPATH)/bin/golangci-lint)

GOOSE_DRIVER  := postgres
GOOSE_DBSTRING ?= $(DATABASE_URL)
GOOSE_DIR     := migrations

.PHONY: build run generate lint vet fmt tidy clean \
        migrate-up migrate-down migrate-status migrate-create \
        test test-unit test-integration dev help

## build: Compile all binaries (flotsamd, flotsam-worker, flotsam)
build:
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o bin/flotsamd ./cmd/flotsamd
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o bin/flotsam-worker ./cmd/flotsam-worker
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o bin/flotsam ./cmd/flotsam

## build-server: Compile flotsamd only
build-server:
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o bin/flotsamd ./cmd/flotsamd

## build-worker: Compile flotsam-worker only
build-worker:
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o bin/flotsam-worker ./cmd/flotsam-worker

## build-cli: Compile flotsam CLI only
build-cli:
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o bin/flotsam ./cmd/flotsam

## run: Run the API server directly (no build step)
run:
	go run $(LDFLAGS) ./cmd/flotsamd

## run-worker: Run the job worker directly (continuous mode)
run-worker:
	go run $(LDFLAGS) ./cmd/flotsam-worker --watch

## generate: Re-run gqlgen code generation
generate:
	go generate ./...

## lint: Run go vet + golangci-lint (includes gosec security checks)
lint:
	go vet ./...
	$(GOLANGCI_LINT) run ./...

## vet: Run go vet
vet:
	go vet ./...

## fmt: Format all Go source files
fmt:
	gofmt -w .

## tidy: Tidy go.mod and go.sum
tidy:
	go mod tidy

## clean: Remove build artifacts
clean:
	rm -rf bin/

## migrate-up: Apply all pending database migrations
migrate-up:
	DATABASE_URL=$(GOOSE_DBSTRING) go run github.com/pressly/goose/v3/cmd/goose \
		-dir $(GOOSE_DIR) $(GOOSE_DRIVER) "$(GOOSE_DBSTRING)" up

## migrate-down: Roll back the most recent migration
migrate-down:
	DATABASE_URL=$(GOOSE_DBSTRING) go run github.com/pressly/goose/v3/cmd/goose \
		-dir $(GOOSE_DIR) $(GOOSE_DRIVER) "$(GOOSE_DBSTRING)" down

## migrate-status: Show migration status
migrate-status:
	go run github.com/pressly/goose/v3/cmd/goose \
		-dir $(GOOSE_DIR) $(GOOSE_DRIVER) "$(GOOSE_DBSTRING)" status

## migrate-create NAME=<name>: Create a new migration file
migrate-create:
	go run github.com/pressly/goose/v3/cmd/goose \
		-dir $(GOOSE_DIR) $(GOOSE_DRIVER) "$(GOOSE_DBSTRING)" create $(NAME) sql

## test: Run all tests with race detector
test:
	go test -race ./...

## test-unit: Run unit tests only (short mode)
test-unit:
	go test -race -short ./...

## test-integration: Run integration tests (requires DATABASE_URL)
test-integration:
	go test -race -run Integration ./...

## dev: Run with air hot-reload (requires: go install github.com/air-verse/air@latest)
dev:
	air

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@grep -E '^## ' Makefile | sed 's/## //' | column -t -s ':'

content: make sure to set up support for creating a database in a testcontainer for local automated and manual testing purposes.  we want realistic data to populate it with as well.  file an issue.

input.name: db-schema
input.prompt:
You are working on issue **flt-it8.1: DB schema — items + pgvector + users** for the Flotsam project.

## Issue Description
Create initial database migration with users, items (+ pgvector embedding column, FTS index), mcp_clients, and audit_log tables. Implement the `internal/db` package with pgxpool connection and goose embedded migration runner.

## What You Must Build

### 1. Migration SQL file: `api/migrations/00001_initial_schema.sql`
Use goose markers (`-- +goose Up` / `-- +goose Down`). The schema is defined in the product spec. Include:
- `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;` and `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;`
- Enum types: `item_type`, `item_status`, `sensitivity_level`
- Tables: `users`, `items` (with pgvector `embedding vector(1536)`, generated `search_vector tsvector`, JSONB columns), `mcp_clients`, `audit_log`
- All indexes from the spec (GIN for tags/metadata/search, ivfflat for embedding, btree for owner/type/created)
- `updated_at` trigger function
- Down migration: drop everything in reverse order

Here is the exact schema from the product spec:

**users table:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE users (
  id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  email         TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  display_name  TEXT,
  password_hash TEXT,
  auth_provider TEXT DEFAULT 'local',
  auth_subject  TEXT,
  settings      JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
```

**items table:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE items (
  id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  owner_id      UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  type          item_type NOT NULL,
  status        item_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
  sensitivity   sensitivity_level NOT NULL DEFAULT 'normal',
  title         TEXT,
  content_text  TEXT,
  content_url   TEXT,
  embedding     vector(1536),
  search_vector tsvector GENERATED ALWAYS AS (
    setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(title, '')), 'A') ||
    setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(content_text, '')), 'B')
  ) STORED,
  tags          TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
  metadata      JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  auto_metadata JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  source        TEXT NOT NULL,
  source_url    TEXT,
  created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  captured_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
```

Enums: `item_type` ('bookmark','voice_note','text_note','drive_doc','highlight','file'), `item_status` ('pending','processing','ready','error'), `sensitivity_level` ('public','normal','sensitive','private')

**mcp_clients table:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE mcp_clients (
  id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  owner_id      UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  name          TEXT NOT NULL,
  api_key_hash  TEXT NOT NULL,
  permissions   JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  requires_approval TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
  active        BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  last_used_at  TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
```

**audit_log table:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE audit_log (
  id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  client_id     UUID REFERENCES mcp_clients(id),
  action        TEXT NOT NULL,
  resource_type TEXT,
  resource_id   UUID,
  request       JSONB,
  response_summary JSONB,
  approved      BOOLEAN,
  created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
```

Indexes on items:
```sql
CREATE INDEX idx_items_owner ON items(owner_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_items_type ON items(owner_id, type);
CREATE INDEX idx_items_created ON items(owner_id, created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_items_tags ON items USING GIN(tags);
CREATE INDEX idx_items_metadata ON items USING GIN(metadata);
CREATE INDEX idx_items_search ON items USING GIN(search_vector);
CREATE INDEX idx_items_embedding ON items USING ivfflat(embedding vector_cosine_ops) WITH (lists = 100);
CREATE INDEX idx_items_sensitivity ON items(owner_id, sensitivity);
```

### 2. Migration embed file: `api/migrations/migrations.go`
```go
package migrations

import "embed"

//go:embed *.sql
var FS embed.FS
```

### 3. `api/internal/db/db.go` — Connection + migration runner
- `Connect(ctx, databaseURL string) (*pgxpool.Pool, error)` — connect with retry (3 attempts, 2s backoff)
- `Migrate(pool *pgxpool.Pool, fs embed.FS) error` — run goose embedded migrations using the pool's connection
- Use `jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool` for the pool
- Use `pressly/goose/v3` for migrations with `goose.SetBaseFS(fs)` and embedded SQL
- Error wrapping: `fmt.Errorf("db: context: %w", err)`
- Structured logging with `log/slog`

### 4. Tests: `api/internal/db/db_test.go`
- Unit tests (run with `-short`): test that Connect fails with invalid URL, test config parsing
- Integration tests (guarded by `testing.Short()` and `DATABASE_URL` env check): test real connection, test migrations apply cleanly
- Target >= 80% coverage

## Current State

You are working on issue **flt-it8.1: DB schema — items + pgvector + users** for the Flotsam project.

## Issue Description
Create initial database migration with users, items (+ pgvector embedding column, FTS index), mcp_clients, and audit_log tables. Implement the `internal/db` package with pgxpool connection and goose embedded migration runner.

## What You Must Build

### 1. Migration SQL file: `api/migrations/00001_initial_schema.sql`
Use goose markers (`-- +goose Up` / `-- +goose Down`). The schema is defined in the product spec. Include:
- `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;` and `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;`
- Enum types: `item_type`, `item_status`, `sensitivity_level`
- Tables: `users`, `items` (with pgvector `embedding vector(1536)`, generated `search_vector tsvector`, JSONB columns), `mcp_clients`, `audit_log`
- All indexes from the spec (GIN for tags/metadata/search, ivfflat for embedding, btree for owner/type/created)
- `updated_at` trigger function
- Down migration: drop everything in reverse order

Here is the exact schema from the product spec:

**users table:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE users (
  id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  email         TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  display_name  TEXT,
  password_hash TEXT,
  auth_provider TEXT DEFAULT 'local',
  auth_subject  TEXT,
  settings      JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
```

**items table:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE items (
  id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  owner_id      UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  type          item_type NOT NULL,
  status        item_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
  sensitivity   sensitivity_level NOT NULL DEFAULT 'normal',
  title         TEXT,
  content_text  TEXT,
  content_url   TEXT,
  embedding     vector(1536),
  search_vector tsvector GENERATED ALWAYS AS (
    setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(title, '')), 'A') ||
    setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(content_text, '')), 'B')
  ) STORED,
  tags          TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
  metadata      JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  auto_metadata JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  source        TEXT NOT NULL,
  source_url    TEXT,
  created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  captured_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
```

Enums: `item_type` ('bookmark','voice_note','text_note','drive_doc','highlight','file'), `item_status` ('pending','processing','ready','error'), `sensitivity_level` ('public','normal','sensitive','private')

**mcp_clients table:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE mcp_clients (
  id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  owner_id      UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  name          TEXT NOT NULL,
  api_key_hash  TEXT NOT NULL,
  permissions   JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  requires_approval TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
  active        BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  last_used_at  TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
```

**audit_log table:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE audit_log (
  id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  client_id     UUID REFERENCES mcp_clients(id),
  action        TEXT NOT NULL,
  resource_type TEXT,
  resource_id   UUID,
  request       JSONB,
  response_summary JSONB,
  approved      BOOLEAN,
  created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
```

Indexes on items:
```sql
CREATE INDEX idx_items_owner ON items(owner_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_items_type ON items(owner_id, type);
CREATE INDEX idx_items_created ON items(owner_id, created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_items_tags ON items USING GIN(tags);
CREATE INDEX idx_items_metadata ON items USING GIN(metadata);
CREATE INDEX idx_items_search ON items USING GIN(search_vector);
CREATE INDEX idx_items_embedding ON items USING ivfflat(embedding vector_cosine_ops) WITH (lists = 100);
CREATE INDEX idx_items_sensitivity ON items(owner_id, sensitivity);
```

### 2. Migration embed file: `api/migrations/migrations.go`
```go
package migrations

import "embed"

//go:embed *.sql
var FS embed.FS
```

### 3. `api/internal/db/db.go` — Connection + migration runner
- `Connect(ctx, databaseURL string) (*pgxpool.Pool, error)` — connect with retry (3 attempts, 2s backoff)
- `Migrate(pool *pgxpool.Pool, fs embed.FS) error` — run goose embedded migrations using the pool's connection
- Use `jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool` for the pool
- Use `pressly/goose/v3` for migrations with `goose.SetBaseFS(fs)` and embedded SQL
- Error wrapping: `fmt.Errorf("db: context: %w", err)`
- Structured logging with `log/slog`

### 4. Tests: `api/internal/db/db_test.go`
- Unit tests (run with `-short`): test that Connect fails with invalid URL, test config parsing
- Integration tests (guarded by `testing.Short()` and `DATABASE_URL` env check): test real connection, test migrations apply cleanly
- Target >= 80% coverage

- `api/internal/db/` directory exists but is empty
- `api/migrations/` directory exists but is empty

input.prompt:
You are working on issue **flt-it8.2: API server scaffold** for the Flotsam project.

## Issue Description
Scaffold the Go API server (flotsamd): config, chi router, middleware (logging, recovery, auth, rate limit), health endpoint, graceful shutdown, goose migration runner. River client in insert-only mode for job enqueue.

## What You Must Build

### 1. `api/internal/config/config.go` — Env var binding
Use `caarlos0/env` struct tags. The config struct should match .env.example:

```go
type Config struct {
    Host              string        `env:"HOST" envDefault:"0.0.0.0"`
    Port              int           `env:"PORT" envDefault:"8081"`
    DatabaseURL       string        `env:"DATABASE_URL,required"`
    JWTSecret         string        `env:"JWT_SECRET,required"`
    S3Endpoint        string        `env:"S3_ENDPOINT"`
    S3Bucket          string        `env:"S3_BUCKET" envDefault:"flotsam-dev"`
    S3AccessKey       string        `env:"S3_ACCESS_KEY"`
    S3SecretKey       string        `env:"S3_SECRET_KEY"`
    S3Region          string        `env:"S3_REGION" envDefault:"us-east-1"`
    S3ForcePathStyle  bool          `env:"S3_FORCE_PATH_STYLE" envDefault:"false"`
    OpenAIAPIKey      string        `env:"OPENAI_API_KEY"`
    OllamaURL         string        `env:"OLLAMA_URL"`
    LogLevel          string        `env:"LOG_LEVEL" envDefault:"info"`
    LogFormat         string        `env:"LOG_FORMAT" envDefault:"json"`
    Env               string        `env:"ENV" envDefault:"development"`
    CORSOrigins       string        `env:"CORS_ORIGINS"`
    GQLPlayground     bool          `env:"GRAPHQL_PLAYGROUND" envDefault:"false"`
    GQLIntrospection  bool          `env:"GRAPHQL_INTROSPECTION" envDefault:"false"`
    MCPEnabled        bool          `env:"MCP_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
    RateLimitEnabled  bool          `env:"RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
    RateLimitGraphQL  int           `env:"RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL" envDefault:"60"`
    RateLimitGraphQLAuth int        `env:"RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL_AUTH" envDefault:"120"`
    RateLimitMCP      int           `env:"RATE_LIMIT_MCP" envDefault:"120"`
    ShutdownTimeout   time.Duration `env:"SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT" envDefault:"15s"`
}

func Load() (*Config, error) // parse from env
func (c *Config) Addr() string // returns "host:port"
```

Write tests in `config_test.go` — test Load with env vars set, test defaults, test required field validation.

### 2. `api/internal/auth/auth.go` — JWT validation
```go
type Claims struct {
    jwt.RegisteredClaims
    UserID     uuid.UUID `json:"uid"`
    Email      string    `json:"email"`
    ClientType string    `json:"ctype"`
}

type Validator interface {
    Validate(tokenString string) (*Claims, error)
}

type HMACValidator struct { secret []byte }

func NewHMACValidator(secret string) *HMACValidator
func (v *HMACValidator) Validate(tokenString string) (*Claims, error)

// Context helpers
func GetClaims(ctx context.Context) *Claims
func WithClaims(ctx context.Context, claims *Claims) context.Context
```

Write tests — test valid token, expired token, wrong secret, malformed token. Use `golang-jwt/jwt/v5`.

### 3. `api/internal/middleware/` — All middleware
Each middleware in its own file:

- `requestid.go` — generates UUID request ID, sets X-Request-ID header, adds to context
- `logging.go` — logs method, path, status, duration using slog; uses request ID from context
- `recovery.go` — recovers from panics, logs stack trace, returns 500
- `auth.go` — two middlewares:
  - `Auth(validator auth.Validator)` — mandatory auth, 401 if missing/invalid
  - `OptionalAuth(validator auth.Validator)` — sets claims if token present, continues if not
  - Extracts `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header
- `ratelimit.go` — per-IP rate limiting using `golang.org/x/time/rate` in-memory token bucket. Configurable limits.

Write tests for each middleware.

### 4. `api/internal/server/server.go` — HTTP server
```go
type Server struct { httpServer *http.Server }

func New(addr string, handler http.Handler) *Server
func (s *Server) Start() error
func (s *Server) Shutdown(ctx context.Context) error
```

Production timeouts: ReadTimeout 15s, WriteTimeout 30s, IdleTimeout 60s.

### 5. `api/internal/router/router.go` — Chi router wiring
```go
type Deps struct {
    Config    *config.Config
    Pool      *pgxpool.Pool
    Auth      auth.Validator
    // River client will be added later
}

func New(deps Deps) chi.Router
```

Wire up:
- Global middleware: RequestID, Logging, Recovery, CORS (if configured)
- Routes:
  - `GET /health` — returns `{"status":"ok","version":"..."}` (no auth)
  - `POST /graphql` — placeholder handler with OptionalAuth (just return 501 for now)
  - `/mcp/*` — placeholder with Auth (just return 501 for now)
  - `POST /upload` — placeholder with Auth (just return 501 for now)

### 6. `api/internal/version/version.go` — Build info
```go
package version

var (
    Version   = "dev"
    BuildTime = "unknown"
)
```
These are set via ldflags in the Makefile (already configured).

### 7. `api/cmd/flotsamd/main.go` — Server entrypoint
Implement the startup sequence from CLAUDE.md:
1. Load `.env` via `joho/godotenv` (no-op if absent)
2. Parse config from env → `config.Load()`
3. Set default slog logger (format/level from config)
4. Connect pgxpool → `db.Connect(ctx, cfg.DatabaseURL)` (import from internal/db, but db.Connect will be provided by flt-it8.1. For now, create a minimal stub in internal/db if needed for compilation, or just import it — the other teammate is building it in parallel)
5. Initialize auth validator → `auth.NewHMACValidator(cfg.JWTSecret)`
6. Build chi router → `router.New(deps)`
7. Start HTTP server; block on SIGINT/SIGTERM; graceful shutdown

**IMPORTANT about internal/db**: Another teammate is building the `internal/db` package (flt-it8.1). You should NOT implement the full db package. For compilation, you may create a minimal `internal/db/db.go` with just `func Connect(ctx context.Context, databaseURL string) (*pgxpool.Pool, error)` that returns an error saying "not implemented" — BUT the better approach is to make flotsamd's main.go work without the db package if possible (e.g., make the pool optional in Deps). Use your judgment — the key thing is your code should compile and tests should pass.

### 8. River client stub
For now, just note in the router.Deps struct that River will be added later. Do NOT implement River — that's flt-it8.3's job. The issue description mentions "River client in insert-only mode" but that will be integrated when the worker issue is done.

## Current State
- `api/go.mod` exists with just `module github.com/ketang/flotsam/api` and `go 1.24.4` — no dependencies yet
- All `internal/` directories exist but are empty
- `cmd/flotsamd/main.go` has a stub that prints "not yet implemented"
- `cmd/flotsam-worker/main.go` and `cmd/flotsam/main.go` also have stubs (don't touch these)

## Quality Gate
Before reporting done, run:
```bash
make api-test-unit && make api-lint
```
Both must pass. If golangci-lint is not installed, `go vet ./...` is sufficient for the lint step.

## Conventions (from CLAUDE.md)
- Error wrapping: `fmt.Errorf("package: context: %w", err)` — always include package prefix
- Logging: `slog.Error("msg", "key", val, "error", err)` — structured, never fmt.Println
- Constructors: return `(T, error)`; caller decides; never panic
- Context: always first parameter
- Package names: single lowercase word matching directory
- File layout: exported types first, unexported helpers below
- Interfaces: named by behaviour (`Validator`, not `IValidator`); defined where consumed
- Dependencies: passed explicitly through structs; no `init()` globals
- SQL: always parameterized

## Commit Convention
```
Scaffold API server: config, middleware, auth, router, server

Implements flotsamd startup: env config, JWT auth, chi router with
middleware (request ID, logging, recovery, rate limit), health endpoint,
and graceful shutdown.

Issues: flt-it8.2
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

## WORKTREE ISOLATION — CRITICAL

### Create your worktree (MANDATORY FIRST STEP)
Before doing ANY other work, create and enter your worktree:

```bash
WORKTREE_NAME="api-scaffold"
REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$WORKTREE_NAME"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/$WORKTREE_NAME"

# Create worktree (if it already exists, just cd into it)
if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```

Then verify you are in the right place:

```bash
echo "=== WORKTREE VERIFICATION ===" && \
echo "CWD: $(pwd)" && \
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)" && \
if echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/'; then \
  echo "✓ WORKTREE VERIFIED"; \
else \
  echo "✗ FATAL: NOT in a worktree! CWD=$(pwd)"; \
  echo "You MUST stop and report this error to the team lead."; \
  exit 1; \
fi
```

If the verification FAILS, do NOT proceed. Report the error to the team lead
immediately via SendMessage and stop all work.

### Rules
1. NEVER cd to the main repo — stay in your worktree directory
2. NEVER run `git checkout` to switch branches — you are already on your branch
3. ALWAYS use relative paths or `$(pwd)` for all operations
4. Before EVERY `git commit` and `git push`, re-verify your CWD is in `.claude/worktrees/`
5. Commit and push ONLY on your worktree branch — never touch main
6. When running `make` targets, run them from your worktree root (your CWD)
7. If ANY command output shows your CWD changed to the main repo, STOP
   immediately, cd back to your worktree, and re-verify

### How to push your branch

```bash
git add <files>
git commit -m "your message"
git push origin HEAD  # pushes your worktree branch, NOT main
```
NEVER run `git push origin main` or `git push origin HEAD:main`.

  - `Auth(validator auth.Validator)` — mandatory auth, 401 if missing/invalid
  - `OptionalAuth(validator auth.Validator)` — sets claims if token present, continues if not
  - Extracts `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header
- `ratelimit.go` — per-IP rate limiting using `golang.org/x/time/rate` in-memory token bucket. Configurable limits.

Write tests for each middleware.

### 4. `api/internal/server/server.go` — HTTP server
```go
type Server struct { httpServer *http.Server }

func New(addr string, handler http.Handler) *Server
func (s *Server) Start() error
func (s *Server) Shutdown(ctx context.Context) error
```

Production timeouts: ReadTimeout 15s, WriteTimeout 30s, IdleTimeout 60s.

### 5. `api/internal/router/router.go` — Chi router wiring
```go
type Deps struct {
    Config    *config.Config
    Pool      *pgxpool.Pool
    Auth      auth.Validator
    // River client will be added later
}

func New(deps Deps) chi.Router
```

Wire up:
- Global middleware: RequestID, Logging, Recovery, CORS (if configured)
- Routes:
  - `GET /health` — returns `{"status":"ok","version":"..."}` (no auth)
  - `POST /graphql` — placeholder handler with OptionalAuth (just return 501 for now)
  - `/mcp/*` — placeholder with Auth (just return 501 for now)
  - `POST /upload` — placeholder with Auth (just return 501 for now)

### 6. `api/internal/version/version.go` — Build info
```go
package version

var (
    Version   = "dev"
    BuildTime = "unknown"
)
```
These are set via ldflags in the Makefile (already configured).

### 7. `api/cmd/flotsamd/main.go` — Server entrypoint
Implement the startup sequence from CLAUDE.md:
1. Load `.env` via `joho/godotenv` (no-op if absent)
2. Parse config from env → `config.Load()`
3. Set default slog logger (format/level from config)
4. Connect pgxpool → `db.Connect(ctx, cfg.DatabaseURL)` (import from internal/db, but db.Connect will be provided by flt-it8.1. For now, create a minimal stub in internal/db if needed for compilation, or just import it — the other teammate is building it in parallel)
5. Initialize auth validator → `auth.NewHMACValidator(cfg.JWTSecret)`
6. Build chi router → `router.New(deps)`
7. Start HTTP server; block on SIGINT/SIGTERM; graceful shutdown

**IMPORTANT about internal/db**: Another teammate is building the `internal/db` package (flt-it8.1). You should NOT implement the full db package. For compilation, you may create a minimal `internal/db/db.go` with just `func Connect(ctx context.Context, databaseURL string) (*pgxpool.Pool, error)` that returns an error saying "not implemented" — BUT the better approach is to make flotsamd's main.go work without the db package if possible (e.g., make the pool optional in Deps). Use your judgment — the key thing is your code should compile and tests should pass.

### 8. River client stub
For now, just note in the router.Deps struct that River will be added later. Do NOT implement River — that's flt-it8.3's job. The issue description mentions "River client in insert-only mode" but that will be integrated when the worker issue is done.

## Current State
- `api/go.mod` exists with just `module github.com/ketang/flotsam/api` and `go 1.24.4` — no dependencies yet
- All `internal/` directories exist but are empty
- `cmd/flotsamd/main.go` has a stub that prints "not yet implemented"
- `cmd/flotsam-worker/main.go` and `cmd/flotsam/main.go` also have stubs (don't touch these)

## Quality Gate
Before reporting done, run:
```bash
make api-test-unit && make api-lint
```
Both must pass. If golangci-lint is not installed, `go vet ./...` is sufficient for the lint step.

## Conventions (from CLAUDE.md)
- Error wrapping: `fmt.Errorf("package: context: %w", err)` — always include package prefix
- Logging: `slog.Error("msg", "key", val, "error", err)` — structured, never fmt.Println
- Constructors: return `(T, error)`; caller decides; never panic
- Context: always first parameter
- Package names: single lowercase word matching directory
- File layout: exported types first, unexported helpers below
- Interfaces: named by behaviour (`Validator`, not `IValidator`); defined where consumed
- Dependencies: passed explicitly through structs; no `init()` globals
- SQL: always parameterized

## Commit Convention
```
Scaffold API server: config, middleware, auth, router, server

Implements flotsamd startup: env config, JWT auth, chi router with
middleware (request ID, logging, recovery, rate limit), health endpoint,
and graceful shutdown.

Issues: flt-it8.2
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

## WORKTREE ISOLATION — CRITICAL

### Create your worktree (MANDATORY FIRST STEP)
Before doing ANY other work, create and enter your worktree:

```bash
WORKTREE_NAME="api-scaffold"
REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$WORKTREE_NAME"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/$WORKTREE_NAME"

# Create worktree (if it already exists, just cd into it)
if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```

Then verify you are in the right place:

```bash
echo "=== WORKTREE VERIFICATION ===" && \
echo "CWD: $(pwd)" && \
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)" && \
if echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/'; then \
  echo "✓ WORKTREE VERIFIED"; \
else \
  echo "✗ FATAL: NOT in a worktree! CWD=$(pwd)"; \
  echo "You MUST stop and report this error to the team lead."; \
  exit 1; \
fi
```

If the verification FAILS, do NOT proceed. Report the error to the team lead
immediately via SendMessage and stop all work.

### Rules
1. NEVER cd to the main repo — stay in your worktree directory
2. NEVER run `git checkout` to switch branches — you are already on your branch
3. ALWAYS use relative paths or `$(pwd)` for all operations
4. Before EVERY `git commit` and `git push`, re-verify your CWD is in `.claude/worktrees/`
5. Commit and push ONLY on your worktree branch — never touch main
6. When running `make` targets, run them from your worktree root (your CWD)
7. If ANY command output shows your CWD changed to the main repo, STOP
   immediately, cd back to your worktree, and re-verify

### How to push your branch
```bash
git add <files>
git commit -m "your message"
git push origin HEAD  # pushes your worktree branch, NOT main
```
NEVER run `git push origin main` or `git push origin HEAD:main`.

## Context
Flotsam needs its initial database schema and Go connection package. The API module (`api/go.mod`) exists but has zero dependencies. No `internal/db/` or `migrations/` files exist yet. We're adapting proven patterns from the kapow project.

## Files to Create

### 1. `api/migrations/00001_initial_schema.sql`
Goose migration with `-- +goose Up` / `-- +goose Down` markers containing:
- Extensions: `vector`, `pg_trgm`
- Enums: `item_type`, `item_status`, `sensitivity_level`
- Tables: `users`, `items` (pgvector + generated tsvector), `mcp_clients`, `audit_log`
- All indexes from the spec (GIN, ivfflat, btree)
- `updated_at` trigger function + triggers on `users` and `items`
- Down migration drops everything in reverse order

### 2. `api/migrations/migrations.go`
Embed wrapper:
```go
package migrations

import "embed"

//go:embed *.sql
var FS embed.FS
```

### 3. `api/internal/db/db.go`
Adapted from kapow's `internal/db/db.go` + `migrate.go`:
- `Connect(ctx, databaseURL) (*pgxpool.Pool, error)` — 3 attempts, exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 3s), ping verification
- `MigrateUp(ctx, databaseURL) error` — goose up via `sql.Open("pgx", ...)` + embedded FS
- `MigrateDown(ctx, databaseURL) error` — goose down (rollback last)
- Error wrapping: `fmt.Errorf("db: ...: %w", err)`
- Structured slog logging

### 4. `api/internal/db/db_test.go`
- **Unit tests** (run with `-short`):
  - `TestConnect_InvalidURL` — Connect fails with garbage URL

- **Integration tests** (skipped when `-short` or no `DATABASE_URL`):
  - `TestIntegrationConnect` — real connection to dev DB
  - `TestIntegrationMigrations` — MigrateUp + MigrateDown on real DB

### 5. `api/go.mod` / `api/go.sum`
Add dependencies via `go get`:
- `github.com/jackc/pgx/v5`
- `github.com/pressly/goose/v3`

1. Create `api/migrations/migrations.go`
2. Create `api/migrations/00001_initial_schema.sql`
3. Create `api/internal/db/db.go`
4. Run `go get` to add dependencies

6. Run `make api-test-unit` and `make api-lint` (or `go vet`)

content.thinking: The user is asking about existing testing and code quality standards. Let me pull from what I've already read in the CLAUDE.md files.

content.text:
From the project's CLAUDE.md files:

## Test Tiers

| Tier | Command | Time | Deps | When |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Quick | `make test-quick` | ~10-20s | None | During dev |
| Standard | `make test-standard` | ~30-60s | None | Before commit |
| Full | `make test-full` | ~2-5min | DB + services | Before merge |

## Coverage & Rules

- **>= 80% line coverage per package** (unit tests). Exempt: `cmd/server`, `internal/router` (wiring-only)
- Tests ship with the code, never as follow-up
- Update existing tests when modifying behaviour
- **DB code must be tested against a real database** — unit tests alone aren't sufficient (pgx encoding issues only surface at runtime)

## Go Testing

- Unit tests: no DB, no network; `go test -race -short ./...`
- Integration tests: prefixed `Integration`, skip when `testing.Short()` or no `DATABASE_URL`
- New GraphQL queries need resolver unit tests **and** integration tests

## Web Testing

- Vitest + `happy-dom`; 80% coverage thresholds enforced
- Use `renderWithMantine()` from `src/test/render.tsx`
- **Every frontend change** must pass `pnpm build` (tsc + vite) and `pnpm lint` (zero warnings)

## Linting

- **Go**: `go vet` + `golangci-lint` (includes gosec security checks)
- **Web**: ESLint with `--max-warnings 0` — zero tolerance for warnings

## Code Conventions (Go)

- Error wrapping with package prefix: `fmt.Errorf("pkg: context: %w", err)`
- Structured logging via `slog` (never `fmt.Println`)
- Context always first param
- No `init()` globals — explicit dependency injection via structs
- SQL always parameterized (`$1`, `$2`) — never `fmt.Sprintf`

The new **flt-it8.19** issue will add testcontainers-go for spinning up real PG+pgvector databases in tests, plus realistic seed data.

    15→
    16→const maxConnectAttempts = 3
    17→
    18→// Connect creates a pgxpool.Pool, pings the database, and returns the pool.
    19→// It retries up to maxConnectAttempts times with exponential backoff.
    20→func Connect(ctx context.Context, databaseURL string) (*pgxpool.Pool, error) {
    21→	cfg, err := pgxpool.ParseConfig(databaseURL)
    22→	if err != nil {
    23→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("db: failed to parse database URL: %w", err)
    24→	}
    25→
    26→	var pool *pgxpool.Pool
    27→	for attempt := 1; attempt <= maxConnectAttempts; attempt++ {
    28→		pool, err = pgxpool.NewWithConfig(ctx, cfg)
    29→		if err != nil {
    30→			slog.Warn("db: connect attempt failed", "attempt", attempt, "error", err)
    31→			time.Sleep(time.Duration(attempt) * time.Second)
    32→			continue
    33→		}
    34→
    35→		if err = pool.Ping(ctx); err != nil {
    36→			pool.Close()
    37→			slog.Warn("db: ping attempt failed", "attempt", attempt, "error", err)
    38→			time.Sleep(time.Duration(attempt) * time.Second)
    39→			continue
    40→		}
    41→
    42→		stat := pool.Stat()
    43→		slog.Info("db: connected",
    44→			"attempt", attempt,
    45→			"max_conns", stat.MaxConns(),
    46→		)
    47→		return pool, nil
    48→	}
    49→
    50→	return nil, fmt.Errorf("db: failed to connect after %d attempts: %w", maxConnectAttempts, err)
    51→}
    52→
    53→// newProvider creates a goose Provider instance using the embedded migrations.
    54→func newProvider(db *sql.DB) (*goose.Provider, error) {
    55→	provider, err := goose.NewProvider(goose.DialectPostgres, db, migrations.FS)
    56→	if err != nil {
    57→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("migrate: create provider: %w", err)
    58→	}
    59→	return provider, nil
    60→}
    61→
    62→// MigrateUp runs all pending goose UP migrations embedded in the binary.
    63→func MigrateUp(ctx context.Context, databaseURL string) error {
    64→	slog.Info("migrate: running up migrations")
    65→	start := time.Now()
    66→
    67→	db, err := sql.Open("pgx", databaseURL)
    68→	if err != nil {
    69→		return fmt.Errorf("migrate: failed to open db: %w", err)
    70→	}
    71→	defer db.Close() //nolint:errcheck // best-effort close
    72→
    73→	provider, err := newProvider(db)
    74→	if err != nil {
    75→		return err
    76→	}
    77→
    78→	results, err := provider.Up(ctx)
    79→	if err != nil {
    80→		slog.Error("migrate: up failed", "error", err, "duration_ms", time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
    81→		return fmt.Errorf("migrate: up: %w", err)
    82→	}
    83→
    84→	slog.Info("migrate: up completed",
    85→		"applied", len(results),
    86→		"duration_ms", time.Since(start).Milliseconds(),
    87→	)
    88→	return nil
    89→}
    90→
    91→// MigrateDown rolls back the most recent goose migration.
    92→func MigrateDown(ctx context.Context, databaseURL string) error {
    93→	slog.Info("migrate: rolling back last migration")
    94→	start := time.Now()
    95→
    96→	db, err := sql.Open("pgx", databaseURL)
    97→	if err != nil {
    98→		return fmt.Errorf("migrate: failed to open db: %w", err)
    99→	}
   100→	defer db.Close() //nolint:errcheck // best-effort close
   101→
   102→	provider, err := newProvider(db)

   104→		return err
   105→	}
   106→
   107→	result, err := provider.Down(ctx)
   108→	if err != nil {
   109→		slog.Error("migrate: down failed", "error", err, "duration_ms", time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
   110→		return fmt.Errorf("migrate: down: %w", err)
   111→	}
   112→
   113→	if result != nil {
   114→		slog.Info("migrate: down completed",
   115→			"rolled_back", result.Source.Path,
   116→			"duration_ms", time.Since(start).Milliseconds(),
   117→		)
   118→	} else {
   119→		slog.Info("migrate: no migrations to roll back")
   120→	}
   121→	return nil
   122→}

- **flt-it8.4** (GraphQL) — `graph/schema/`, `graph/resolver/`, `graph/model/`, `graph/generated/`, `internal/router/` (wire GraphQL handler), `internal/item/` (CRUD). Big scope.
- **flt-it8.5** (Auth) — `internal/auth/` (add password hashing, registration/login service), needs GraphQL mutations for login/register. 

The auth work overlaps with GraphQL on schema and resolvers, so those two need to be ordered sequentially. S3 storage and the testcontainer utilities are both isolated from the rest. This means I can run the background jobs and GraphQL work in parallel, but auth has to follow GraphQL.

Actually, looking closer at what's left in the auth task — password hashing, user registration/login service, and token generation — most of that lives outside GraphQL. The mutations for login/register would touch the schema, but the core auth logic could potentially proceed independently if we're careful about the interface.

But realistically, flt-it8.4 needs to bootstrap gqlgen entirely first with the schema file, generated resolvers, and the basic setup. Then flt-it8.5 can layer auth mutations on top of that foundation. So they need to be sequential.

I'll stick with 4 parallel tasks: flt-it8.3, flt-it8.4, flt-it8.6, and flt-it8.19 — they're all genuinely independent. Now I should write out the detailed prompts for each one.

1→package main
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"fmt"
     5→	"os"
     6→)

1→package main
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"log/slog"
     6→	"os"
     7→	"os/signal"
     8→	"syscall"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/joho/godotenv"
    11→
    12→	"github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/auth"
    13→	"github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/config"
    14→	"github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/db"
    15→	"github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/router"
    16→	"github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/server"
    17→	"github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/version"
    18→)
    19→
    20→func main() {
    21→	// 1. Load .env (no-op if file is absent — dev convenience only)
    22→	_ = godotenv.Load()
    23→
    24→	// 2. Parse and validate configuration
    25→	cfg, err := config.Load()
    26→	if err != nil {
    27→		slog.Error("failed to load config", "error", err)
    28→		os.Exit(1)
    29→	}
    30→
    31→	// 3. Configure structured logger
    32→	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(cfg.LogHandler()))
    33→	slog.Info("starting flotsam api",
    34→		"version", version.Version,
    35→		"build_time", version.BuildTime,
    36→		"env", cfg.Env,
    37→	)
    38→
    39→	// 4. Connect to database
    40→	ctx := context.Background()
    41→	pool, err := db.Connect(ctx, cfg.DatabaseURL)
    42→	if err != nil {
    43→		slog.Error("failed to connect to database", "error", err)
    44→		os.Exit(1) //nolint:gocritic // fatal startup error
    45→	}
    46→	defer pool.Close()
    47→
    48→	// 5. Initialize auth validator
    49→	validator := auth.NewHMACValidator(cfg.JWTSecret)
    50→	slog.Info("auth: using HS256 JWT validator")
    51→
    52→	// 6. Build router
    53→	r := router.New(router.Deps{
    54→		Config:    cfg,
    55→		Pool:      pool,
    56→		Validator: validator,
    57→	})
    58→
    59→	// 7. Start HTTP server
    60→	srv := server.New(cfg.Addr(), r)
    61→
    62→	sigCh := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
    63→	signal.Notify(sigCh, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
    64→
    65→	go func() {
    66→		slog.Info("server: listening", "addr", cfg.Addr())
    67→		if err := srv.Start(); err != nil {
    68→			slog.Info("server: stopped", "reason", err)
    69→		}
    70→	}()
    71→
    72→	// 8. Block until signal received
    73→	sig := <-sigCh
    74→	slog.Info("signal received", "signal", sig)
    75→
    76→	// 9. Graceful shutdown
    77→	shutdownCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), cfg.ShutdownTimeout)
    78→	defer cancel()
    79→
    80→	slog.Info("server: shutting down", "timeout", cfg.ShutdownTimeout)
    81→	if err := srv.Shutdown(shutdownCtx); err != nil {
    82→		slog.Error("server: shutdown error", "error", err)
    83→		os.Exit(1)
    84→	}
    85→	slog.Info("server: shutdown complete")
    86→}
    87→

1→package router
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"encoding/json"
     5→	"log/slog"
     6→	"net/http"
     7→	"strings"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5"
    10→	chimiddleware "github.com/go-chi/chi/v5/middleware"
    11→	"github.com/go-chi/cors"
    12→	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
    13→
    14→	"github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/auth"
    15→	"github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/config"
    16→	flotsammiddleware "github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/middleware"
    17→	"github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/version"
    18→)
    19→
    20→// Deps holds the dependencies needed to construct the router.
    21→type Deps struct {
    22→	Config    *config.Config
    23→	Pool      *pgxpool.Pool
    24→	Validator auth.Validator
    25→	// River client will be added when flt-it8.3 is implemented.
    26→}
    27→
    28→// New constructs the chi router with all middleware, routes, and handlers.
    29→func New(deps Deps) http.Handler {
    30→	r := chi.NewRouter()
    31→
    32→	// Global middleware (applied to every request)
    33→	r.Use(flotsammiddleware.RequestID)
    34→	r.Use(flotsammiddleware.Logging)
    35→	r.Use(flotsammiddleware.Recovery)
    36→	r.Use(chimiddleware.RealIP)
    37→	r.Use(chimiddleware.StripSlashes)
    38→
    39→	// CORS (if configured)
    40→	if deps.Config.CORSOrigins != "" {
    41→		origins := parseCORSOrigins(deps.Config.CORSOrigins)
    42→		r.Use(cors.Handler(cors.Options{
    43→			AllowedOrigins:   origins,
    44→			AllowedMethods:   []string{"GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE", "OPTIONS"},
    45→			AllowedHeaders:   []string{"Accept", "Authorization", "Content-Type"},
    46→			AllowCredentials: true,
    47→			MaxAge:           300,
    48→		}))
    49→	}
    50→
    51→	// Health endpoint — no auth
    52→	r.Get("/health", healthHandler)
    53→
    54→	// GraphQL endpoint — optional auth + rate limit
    55→	r.Route("/graphql", func(r chi.Router) {
    56→		r.Use(flotsammiddleware.OptionalAuth(deps.Validator))
    57→		if deps.Config.RateLimitEnabled {
    58→			rl := flotsammiddleware.NewRateLimiter(deps.Config.RateLimitGraphQL)
    59→			r.Use(rl.Limit)
    60→		}
    61→		r.Post("/", placeholderHandler("graphql"))
    62→		r.Get("/", placeholderHandler("graphql"))
    63→	})
    64→
    65→	// MCP endpoint — mandatory auth + rate limit
    66→	if deps.Config.MCPEnabled {
    67→		r.Route("/mcp", func(r chi.Router) {
    68→			r.Use(flotsammiddleware.Auth(deps.Validator))
    69→			if deps.Config.RateLimitEnabled {
    70→				rl := flotsammiddleware.NewRateLimiter(deps.Config.RateLimitMCP)
    71→				r.Use(rl.Limit)
    72→			}
    73→			r.Handle("/*", placeholderHandler("mcp"))
    74→		})
    75→		slog.Info("router: MCP endpoint enabled at /mcp")
    76→	}
    77→
    78→	// Upload endpoint — mandatory auth
    79→	r.Route("/upload", func(r chi.Router) {
    80→		r.Use(flotsammiddleware.Auth(deps.Validator))
    81→		r.Post("/", placeholderHandler("upload"))
    82→	})
    83→
    84→	slog.Info("router: initialized",
    85→		"cors_origins", deps.Config.CORSOrigins,
    86→		"mcp_enabled", deps.Config.MCPEnabled,
    87→		"rate_limit_enabled", deps.Config.RateLimitEnabled,
    88→	)
    89→
    90→	return r
    91→}
    92→
    93→func healthHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, _ *http.Request) {
    94→	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
    95→	_ = json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{
    96→		"status":  "ok",
    97→		"version": version.Version,
    98→	})
    99→}
   100→
   101→func placeholderHandler(name string) http.HandlerFunc {
   102→	return func(w http.ResponseWriter, _ *http.Request) {
   103→		http.Error(w, name+" endpoint not yet implemented", http.StatusNotImplemented)
   104→	}
   105→}
   106→
   107→func parseCORSOrigins(s string) []string {
   108→	parts := strings.Split(s, ",")
   109→	origins := make([]string, 0, len(parts))
   110→	for _, p := range parts {
   111→		p = strings.TrimSpace(p)
   112→		if p != "" {
   113→			origins = append(origins, p)
   114→		}
   115→	}
   116→	return origins
   117→}
   118→

1→package config
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"fmt"
     5→	"log/slog"
     6→	"os"
     7→	"time"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/caarlos0/env/v11"
    10→)
    11→
    12→// Config holds all application configuration loaded from environment variables.
    13→type Config struct {
    14→	Host string `env:"HOST" envDefault:"0.0.0.0"`
    15→	Port int    `env:"PORT" envDefault:"8081"`
    16→
    17→	DatabaseURL string `env:"DATABASE_URL,required"`
    18→	JWTSecret   string `env:"JWT_SECRET,required"` //nolint:gosec // config field name, not a hardcoded secret
    19→
    20→	S3Endpoint       string `env:"S3_ENDPOINT"`
    21→	S3Bucket         string `env:"S3_BUCKET" envDefault:"flotsam-dev"`
    22→	S3AccessKey      string `env:"S3_ACCESS_KEY"`
    23→	S3SecretKey      string `env:"S3_SECRET_KEY"`
    24→	S3Region         string `env:"S3_REGION" envDefault:"us-east-1"`
    25→	S3ForcePathStyle bool   `env:"S3_FORCE_PATH_STYLE" envDefault:"false"`
    26→
    27→	OpenAIAPIKey string `env:"OPENAI_API_KEY"`
    28→	OllamaURL    string `env:"OLLAMA_URL"`
    29→
    30→	LogLevel  string `env:"LOG_LEVEL" envDefault:"info"`
    31→	LogFormat string `env:"LOG_FORMAT" envDefault:"json"`
    32→
    33→	Env string `env:"ENV" envDefault:"development"`
    34→
    35→	CORSOrigins string `env:"CORS_ORIGINS"`
    36→
    37→	GQLPlayground    bool `env:"GRAPHQL_PLAYGROUND" envDefault:"false"`
    38→	GQLIntrospection bool `env:"GRAPHQL_INTROSPECTION" envDefault:"false"`
    39→
    40→	MCPEnabled bool `env:"MCP_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
    41→
    42→	RateLimitEnabled     bool `env:"RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
    43→	RateLimitGraphQL     int  `env:"RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL" envDefault:"60"`
    44→	RateLimitGraphQLAuth int  `env:"RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL_AUTH" envDefault:"120"`
    45→	RateLimitMCP         int  `env:"RATE_LIMIT_MCP" envDefault:"120"`
    46→
    47→	ShutdownTimeout time.Duration `env:"SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT" envDefault:"15s"`
    48→}
    49→
    50→// Load parses and validates configuration from environment variables.
    51→func Load() (*Config, error) {
    52→	cfg := &Config{}
    53→	if err := env.Parse(cfg); err != nil {
    54→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("config: %w", err)
    55→	}
    56→	return cfg, nil
    57→}
    58→
    59→// Addr returns the host:port bind address.
    60→func (c *Config) Addr() string {
    61→	return fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", c.Host, c.Port)
    62→}
    63→
    64→// ParseLogLevel converts a log level string to the corresponding slog.Level.
    65→func ParseLogLevel(s string) slog.Level {
    66→	switch s {
    67→	case "trace":
    68→		return slog.Level(-8) // custom TRACE level
    69→	case "debug":
    70→		return slog.LevelDebug
    71→	case "warn":
    72→		return slog.LevelWarn
    73→	case "error":
    74→		return slog.LevelError
    75→	default:
    76→		return slog.LevelInfo
    77→	}
    78→}
    79→
    80→// LogHandler returns an slog.Handler configured per LOG_LEVEL and LOG_FORMAT.
    81→func (c *Config) LogHandler() slog.Handler {
    82→	level := ParseLogLevel(c.LogLevel)
    83→	opts := &slog.HandlerOptions{
    84→		Level:     level,
    85→		AddSource: c.Env == "development",
    86→	}
    87→
    88→	if c.LogFormat == "text" {
    89→		return slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stderr, opts)
    90→	}
    91→	return slog.NewJSONHandler(os.Stderr, opts)
    92→}
    93→

input.prompt:
You are working on issue **flt-it8.3: Background job processing** for the Flotsam project.

## Issue Description
Implement flotsam-worker binary: River job processor with --watch (continuous) and --once (run-and-exit) modes. Define job types: PageFetch, Transcribe, Embed, Classify, S3Upload. flotsamd --with-worker flag to auto-fork worker as child process.

## What You Must Build

### 1. `api/internal/worker/` — Job types and registry

Create one file per job type, plus a registry:

**`api/internal/worker/registry.go`**
- `func Workers() *river.Workers` — returns a workers collection with all job types registered
- Central registration point

**Job type files** (each with `Args` struct + `Worker` struct implementing `river.Worker[Args]`):

- `api/internal/worker/page_fetch.go` — `PageFetchArgs{ItemID uuid.UUID, URL string}`, `PageFetchWorker`
- `api/internal/worker/transcribe.go` — `TranscribeArgs{ItemID uuid.UUID, AudioURL string, Provider string}`, `TranscribeWorker`  
- `api/internal/worker/embed.go` — `EmbedArgs{ItemID uuid.UUID, Content string}`, `EmbedWorker`
- `api/internal/worker/classify.go` — `ClassifyArgs{ItemID uuid.UUID, Content string}`, `ClassifyWorker`
- `api/internal/worker/s3upload.go` — `S3UploadArgs{ItemID uuid.UUID, Key string, ContentType string}`, `S3UploadWorker`

Each worker's `Work()` method should be a **stub** — log the job details and return nil (the real implementations come in later issues). The important thing is the River worker interface is properly implemented.

Each Args struct needs `Kind() string` method returning a descriptive kind like `"page_fetch"`, `"transcribe"`, etc.

### 2. `api/cmd/flotsam-worker/main.go` — Worker binary

Replace the stub with a real implementation:
1. Load `.env` via godotenv
2. Parse config → `config.Load()`
3. Set up slog logger
4. Connect to DB → `db.Connect(ctx, cfg.DatabaseURL)`
5. Parse flags: `--watch` (continuous) and `--once` (run pending and exit)
6. Create River client with workers registered
7. Start the River client
8. In `--watch` mode: block on SIGINT/SIGTERM, graceful shutdown
9. In `--once` mode: process pending jobs and exit (or use a brief polling window)

Use `flag` package for CLI flags. Default to `--once` if neither flag is specified.

### 3. Modify `api/cmd/flotsamd/main.go` — Add `--with-worker` flag

Add a `--with-worker` flag that, when set, forks `flotsam-worker --watch` as a child process:
```go
if withWorker {
    cmd := exec.Command("flotsam-worker", "--watch")
    cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
    cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
    if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {
        slog.Error("failed to start worker", "error", err)
    } else {
        slog.Info("worker: started as child process", "pid", cmd.Process.Pid)
        defer cmd.Process.Kill()
    }
}
```

Also add River client initialization in insert-only mode to flotsamd, so the API server can enqueue jobs:
```go
riverClient, err := river.NewClient(riverpgxv5.New(pool), &river.Config{})
```
Add the River client to `router.Deps` (add a `RiverClient` field).

### 4. Modify `api/internal/router/router.go` — Add RiverClient to Deps

Add a `RiverClient` field to the `Deps` struct. The River client type is `*river.Client[pgx.Tx]` from `riverqueue/river`. Import it but don't use it in any routes yet.

### 5. Tests: `api/internal/worker/worker_test.go`

- Test that `Workers()` returns a non-nil workers collection
- Test that each Args struct returns the correct `Kind()` string
- Test worker stubs don't error (call Work() with mock context)

## Existing Code Context

**`api/cmd/flotsamd/main.go`** — current startup sequence (load config, connect DB, init auth, build router, start server, signal handling). You need to add --with-worker flag and River client init.

**`api/internal/router/router.go`** — has `Deps` struct with Config, Pool, Validator. Add RiverClient field.

**`api/go.mod`** — Go 1.25.0, already has pgx/v5, goose, chi, jwt, etc. You'll add `riverqueue/river`.

**`api/internal/config/config.go`** — Config struct with all env vars. No changes needed here.

## Quality Gate
Before reporting done, run:
```bash
make api-test-unit && make api-lint
```
If golangci-lint is not installed, `go vet ./...` is sufficient.

## Conventions (from CLAUDE.md)
- Error wrapping: `fmt.Errorf("worker: context: %w", err)`
- Logging: `slog.Info/Error/Warn("msg", "key", val)`
- Context: always first parameter
- No `init()` globals — explicit dependency injection

## Commit Convention
```
Add River background job processing and worker binary

Implements flotsam-worker with --watch/--once modes and five job type
stubs (PageFetch, Transcribe, Embed, Classify, S3Upload). Adds
--with-worker flag to flotsamd for auto-forking worker process.

Issues: flt-it8.3
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

## WORKTREE ISOLATION — CRITICAL

### Create your worktree (MANDATORY FIRST STEP)
Before doing ANY other work, create and enter your worktree:

```bash
WORKTREE_NAME="bg-jobs"
REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$WORKTREE_NAME"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/$WORKTREE_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```

Then verify:
```bash
echo "=== WORKTREE VERIFICATION ===" && \
echo "CWD: $(pwd)" && \
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)" && \
if echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/'; then \
  echo "✓ WORKTREE VERIFIED"; \
else \
  echo "✗ FATAL: NOT in a worktree! CWD=$(pwd)"; exit 1; \
fi
```

### Rules
1. NEVER cd to the main repo — stay in your worktree
2. NEVER run `git checkout` to switch branches
3. Before EVERY git commit, re-verify CWD is in `.claude/worktrees/`
4. Push with `git push origin HEAD`

3. Set up slog logger
4. Connect to DB → `db.Connect(ctx, cfg.DatabaseURL)`
5. Parse flags: `--watch` (continuous) and `--once` (run pending and exit)
6. Create River client with workers registered
7. Start the River client
8. In `--watch` mode: block on SIGINT/SIGTERM, graceful shutdown
9. In `--once` mode: process pending jobs and exit (or use a brief polling window)

Use `flag` package for CLI flags. Default to `--once` if neither flag is specified.

### 3. Modify `api/cmd/flotsamd/main.go` — Add `--with-worker` flag

Add a `--with-worker` flag that, when set, forks `flotsam-worker --watch` as a child process:
```go
if withWorker {
    cmd := exec.Command("flotsam-worker", "--watch")
    cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
    cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
    if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {
        slog.Error("failed to start worker", "error", err)
    } else {
        slog.Info("worker: started as child process", "pid", cmd.Process.Pid)
        defer cmd.Process.Kill()
    }
}
```

Also add River client initialization in insert-only mode to flotsamd, so the API server can enqueue jobs:
```go
riverClient, err := river.NewClient(riverpgxv5.New(pool), &river.Config{})
```
Add the River client to `router.Deps` (add a `RiverClient` field).

### 4. Modify `api/internal/router/router.go` — Add RiverClient to Deps

Add a `RiverClient` field to the `Deps` struct. The River client type is `*river.Client[pgx.Tx]` from `riverqueue/river`. Import it but don't use it in any routes yet.

### 5. Tests: `api/internal/worker/worker_test.go`

- Test that `Workers()` returns a non-nil workers collection
- Test that each Args struct returns the correct `Kind()` string
- Test worker stubs don't error (call Work() with mock context)

## Existing Code Context

**`api/cmd/flotsamd/main.go`** — current startup sequence (load config, connect DB, init auth, build router, start server, signal handling). You need to add --with-worker flag and River client init.

**`api/internal/router/router.go`** — has `Deps` struct with Config, Pool, Validator. Add RiverClient field.

**`api/go.mod`** — Go 1.25.0, already has pgx/v5, goose, chi, jwt, etc. You'll add `riverqueue/river`.

**`api/internal/config/config.go`** — Config struct with all env vars. No changes needed here.

## Quality Gate
Before reporting done, run:
```bash
make api-test-unit && make api-lint
```
If golangci-lint is not installed, `go vet ./...` is sufficient.

## Conventions (from CLAUDE.md)
- Error wrapping: `fmt.Errorf("worker: context: %w", err)`
- Logging: `slog.Info/Error/Warn("msg", "key", val)`
- Context: always first parameter
- No `init()` globals — explicit dependency injection

## Commit Convention
```
Add River background job processing and worker binary

Implements flotsam-worker with --watch/--once modes and five job type
stubs (PageFetch, Transcribe, Embed, Classify, S3Upload). Adds
--with-worker flag to flotsamd for auto-forking worker process.

Issues: flt-it8.3
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

## WORKTREE ISOLATION — CRITICAL

### Create your worktree (MANDATORY FIRST STEP)
Before doing ANY other work, create and enter your worktree:

```bash
WORKTREE_NAME="bg-jobs"
REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$WORKTREE_NAME"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/$WORKTREE_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```

Then verify:
```bash
echo "=== WORKTREE VERIFICATION ===" && \
echo "CWD: $(pwd)" && \
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)" && \
if echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/'; then \
  echo "✓ WORKTREE VERIFIED"; \
else \
  echo "✗ FATAL: NOT in a worktree! CWD=$(pwd)"; exit 1; \
fi
```

### Rules
1. NEVER cd to the main repo — stay in your worktree
2. NEVER run `git checkout` to switch branches
3. Before EVERY git commit, re-verify CWD is in `.claude/worktrees/`
4. Push with `git push origin HEAD`

input.name: graphql
input.prompt:
You are working on issue **flt-it8.4: GraphQL schema + resolvers** for the Flotsam project.

## Issue Description
Define GraphQL SDL (items, search, mutations) and implement gqlgen resolvers for Item CRUD and search.

## What You Must Build

### 1. Set up gqlgen — `api/gqlgen.yml`

Create the gqlgen config file. Use schema-first approach:
```yaml
schema:
  - graph/schema/*.graphql

exec:
  filename: graph/generated/generated.go
  package: generated

model:
  filename: graph/model/models_gen.go
  package: model

resolver:
  layout: follow-schema
  dir: graph/resolver
  package: resolver

autobind:
  - github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/graph/model

models:
  ID:
    model:
      - github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql.ID
  DateTime:
    model:
      - github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql.Time
  JSON:
    model:
      - github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql.Map
```

Add `//go:generate go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen generate` to a `graph/generate.go` file.

### 2. GraphQL SDL — `api/graph/schema/`

Create SDL files defining the schema from the product spec:

**`api/graph/schema/schema.graphql`** — base schema with Query and Mutation types
**`api/graph/schema/item.graphql`** — Item type, enums, inputs, filters

Types to define (from product spec):
```graphql
scalar DateTime
scalar JSON

type Item {
  id: ID!
  type: ItemType!
  status: ItemStatus!
  sensitivity: SensitivityLevel!
  title: String
  contentText: String
  contentUrl: String
  tags: [String!]!
  metadata: JSON!
  autoMetadata: JSON!
  source: String!
  sourceUrl: String
  createdAt: DateTime!
  updatedAt: DateTime!
  capturedAt: DateTime!
}

enum ItemType { BOOKMARK, VOICE_NOTE, TEXT_NOTE, DRIVE_DOC, HIGHLIGHT, FILE }
enum ItemStatus { PENDING, PROCESSING, READY, ERROR }
enum SensitivityLevel { PUBLIC, NORMAL, SENSITIVE, PRIVATE }

type SearchResult {
  items: [Item!]!
  total: Int!
  hasMore: Boolean!
}

input ItemFilter {
  types: [ItemType!]
  tags: [String!]
  sensitivity: [SensitivityLevel!]
  source: String
  dateFrom: DateTime
  dateTo: DateTime
  status: [ItemStatus!]
}

enum ItemSortField { CREATED_AT, UPDATED_AT, CAPTURED_AT, TITLE }
enum SortDirection { ASC, DESC }
input ItemSort {
  field: ItemSortField!
  direction: SortDirection!
}

input BookmarkInput {
  url: String!
  title: String
  selection: String
  referrer: String
  tags: [String!]
  sensitivity: SensitivityLevel
}

input NoteInput {
  title: String
  content: String!
  tags: [String!]
  sensitivity: SensitivityLevel
}

input UpdateItemInput {
  title: String
  tags: [String!]
  sensitivity: SensitivityLevel
}

type Query {
  item(id: ID!): Item
  items(filter: ItemFilter, limit: Int, offset: Int, sort: ItemSort): SearchResult!
  search(query: String!, filter: ItemFilter, limit: Int, threshold: Float): SearchResult!
}

type Mutation {
  captureBookmark(input: BookmarkInput!): Item!
  captureNote(input: NoteInput!): Item!
  updateItem(id: ID!, input: UpdateItemInput!): Item!
  deleteItem(id: ID!): Boolean!
  updateSensitivity(id: ID!, sensitivity: SensitivityLevel!): Item!
  tagItem(id: ID!, tags: [String!]!): Item!
}
```

### 3. `api/internal/item/` — Item service

Create the item service package that resolvers will call:

**`api/internal/item/service.go`**
```go
type Service struct {
    pool *pgxpool.Pool
}

func NewService(pool *pgxpool.Pool) *Service

func (s *Service) GetByID(ctx context.Context, ownerID, itemID uuid.UUID) (*Item, error)
func (s *Service) List(ctx context.Context, ownerID uuid.UUID, filter *Filter, limit, offset int, sort *Sort) (*SearchResult, error)
func (s *Service) Search(ctx context.Context, ownerID uuid.UUID, query string, filter *Filter, limit int, threshold float64) (*SearchResult, error)
func (s *Service) CaptureBookmark(ctx context.Context, ownerID uuid.UUID, input BookmarkInput) (*Item, error)
func (s *Service) CaptureNote(ctx context.Context, ownerID uuid.UUID, input NoteInput) (*Item, error)
func (s *Service) Update(ctx context.Context, ownerID, itemID uuid.UUID, input UpdateInput) (*Item, error)
func (s *Service) Delete(ctx context.Context, ownerID, itemID uuid.UUID) error
func (s *Service) UpdateSensitivity(ctx context.Context, ownerID, itemID uuid.UUID, sensitivity string) (*Item, error)
func (s *Service) Tag(ctx context.Context, ownerID, itemID uuid.UUID, tags []string) (*Item, error)
```

**`api/internal/item/model.go`** — Go types for Item, Filter, Sort, SearchResult, inputs

**`api/internal/item/service_test.go`** — Unit tests using mock/stub DB (test input validation, business logic). Integration tests guarded by `testing.Short()`.

The service methods should use real pgx queries with parameterized SQL (`$1`, `$2`, etc.). For search, use the `search_vector` tsvector column with `ts_rank`. For semantic search, use pgvector `<=>` cosine distance (but the embedding parameter will be nil for now since we don't have the embedding provider yet — return an error if no embedding is available).

### 4. Resolver implementations — `api/graph/resolver/`

**`api/graph/resolver/resolver.go`**
```go
type Resolver struct {
    Items *item.Service
}
```

**`api/graph/resolver/schema.resolvers.go`** — will be partially generated by gqlgen, you fill in the implementations:
- Each resolver extracts `auth.GetClaims(ctx)` to get the owner ID
- Mutations require auth (return error if claims are nil)
- Queries allow optional auth for now (but item access requires auth)
- Map between GraphQL model types and internal item types

### 5. Wire GraphQL into router

Modify `api/internal/router/router.go`:
- Replace the placeholder GraphQL handler with the real gqlgen handler
- Import `graph/generated` and `graph/resolver`
- Add `Items *item.Service` to `Deps` or construct it in `New()`
- Wire up the GraphQL Playground if `GQLPlayground` is enabled

### 6. Wire item service in `api/cmd/flotsamd/main.go`

Add `item.NewService(pool)` and pass to router deps.

### 7. Run code generation

After creating the SDL and gqlgen.yml:
```bash
go get github.com/99designs/gqlgen@latest
go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen generate
```

This generates `graph/generated/generated.go` and `graph/model/models_gen.go`. Then implement the resolver methods.

## Existing Code Context

**`api/internal/router/router.go`** — has Deps struct, placeholder handler for `/graphql`. Replace with real gqlgen handler.

**`api/cmd/flotsamd/main.go`** — startup sequence. Add item service init and pass to router.

**`api/internal/auth/context.go`** — `GetClaims(ctx) *Claims` for extracting authenticated user.

**`api/internal/auth/auth.go`** — `Claims` struct with `UserID uuid.UUID`, `Email string`.

**Database schema** (from migration): items table with columns id, owner_id, type, status, sensitivity, title, content_text, content_url, embedding, search_vector, tags, metadata, auto_metadata, source, source_url, created_at, updated_at, captured_at. Enums: item_type, item_status, sensitivity_level.

## Quality Gate
Before reporting done, run:
```bash
make api-test-unit && make api-lint
```
If golangci-lint is not installed, `go vet ./...` is sufficient.

## Important Notes
- **Never edit** generated files manually — run gqlgen generate
- All SQL must use parameterized queries (`$1`, `$2`)
- All item queries MUST filter by `owner_id` (multi-tenant security)
- Use `pgx` directly — no ORM

## Conventions
- Error wrapping: `fmt.Errorf("item: context: %w", err)` 
- Logging: structured slog
- Context: always first parameter
- SQL: always parameterized

## Commit Convention
```
Add GraphQL schema and item CRUD resolvers

Implements gqlgen schema-first GraphQL with Item types, search,
and mutation resolvers backed by pgx item service.

Issues: flt-it8.4
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

## WORKTREE ISOLATION — CRITICAL

### Create your worktree (MANDATORY FIRST STEP)
```bash
WORKTREE_NAME="graphql"
REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$WORKTREE_NAME"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/$WORKTREE_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi
cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```

Then verify:
```bash
echo "=== WORKTREE VERIFICATION ===" && \
echo "CWD: $(pwd)" && \
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)" && \
if echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/'; then \
  echo "✓ WORKTREE VERIFIED"; \
else \
  echo "✗ FATAL: NOT in a worktree! CWD=$(pwd)"; exit 1; \
fi
```

### Rules
1. NEVER cd to the main repo — stay in your worktree
2. NEVER run `git checkout` to switch branches
3. Before EVERY git commit, re-verify CWD is in `.claude/worktrees/`
4. Push with `git push origin HEAD`

input.name: s3-storage
input.prompt:
You are working on issue **flt-it8.6: S3 storage integration** for the Flotsam project.

## Issue Description
S3 client abstraction (works with MinIO dev and R2/S3 prod). Upload/download/delete operations. Key hierarchy by owner_id/type/item_id.

## What You Must Build

### 1. `api/internal/storage/storage.go` — S3 client abstraction

```go
type Client struct {
    s3Client *s3.Client
    bucket   string
}

type Config struct {
    Endpoint       string
    Bucket         string
    AccessKey      string
    SecretKey      string
    Region         string
    ForcePathStyle bool
}

func New(cfg Config) (*Client, error)

// Upload uploads content to S3 at the given key.
func (c *Client) Upload(ctx context.Context, key string, body io.Reader, contentType string) error

// Download retrieves content from S3 at the given key.
func (c *Client) Download(ctx context.Context, key string) (io.ReadCloser, error)

// Delete removes an object from S3.
func (c *Client) Delete(ctx context.Context, key string) error

// Exists checks if an object exists at the given key.
func (c *Client) Exists(ctx context.Context, key string) (bool, error)

// PresignedURL generates a pre-signed URL for temporary access.
func (c *Client) PresignedURL(ctx context.Context, key string, expiry time.Duration) (string, error)
```

Use `aws-sdk-go-v2` (not v1). The client should work with:
- MinIO (dev) — needs `ForcePathStyle: true`, custom endpoint
- Cloudflare R2 (prod) — S3-compatible, custom endpoint
- AWS S3 — standard config

### 2. `api/internal/storage/keys.go` — Key hierarchy helpers

```go
// KeyFor generates the S3 key for an item's content.
// Pattern: {owner_id}/{type}/{item_id}/{filename}
func KeyFor(ownerID uuid.UUID, itemType string, itemID uuid.UUID, filename string) string

// BookmarkKey returns the key for a bookmark's fetched HTML content.
func BookmarkKey(ownerID, itemID uuid.UUID) string  // → {owner_id}/bookmarks/{item_id}/content.html

// AudioKey returns the key for a voice note's audio file.
func AudioKey(ownerID, itemID uuid.UUID, format string) string  // → {owner_id}/voice_notes/{item_id}/audio.{format}

// FileKey returns the key for a generic file upload.
func FileKey(ownerID, itemID uuid.UUID, filename string) string  // → {owner_id}/files/{item_id}/{filename}
```

### 3. Tests

## Issue Description

## What You Must Build

### 1. `api/internal/storage/storage.go` — S3 client abstraction

```go
type Client struct {
    s3Client *s3.Client
    bucket   string
}

type Config struct {
    Endpoint       string
    Bucket         string
    AccessKey      string
    SecretKey      string
    Region         string
    ForcePathStyle bool
}

func New(cfg Config) (*Client, error)

// Upload uploads content to S3 at the given key.
func (c *Client) Upload(ctx context.Context, key string, body io.Reader, contentType string) error

// Download retrieves content from S3 at the given key.
func (c *Client) Download(ctx context.Context, key string) (io.ReadCloser, error)

// Delete removes an object from S3.
func (c *Client) Delete(ctx context.Context, key string) error

// Exists checks if an object exists at the given key.
func (c *Client) Exists(ctx context.Context, key string) (bool, error)

// PresignedURL generates a pre-signed URL for temporary access.

```

input.prompt:
You are working on issue **flt-it8.19: Testcontainer DB + realistic seed data** for the Flotsam project.

## Issue Description
Set up testcontainers-go for PostgreSQL 16 + pgvector so integration tests and local dev can spin up a real database automatically. Include: (1) a shared test helper that creates a pgvector-enabled container and runs goose migrations, (2) a seed data package with realistic sample users, items of each type (bookmarks, voice notes, text notes), MCP clients, and audit log entries, (3) make target for populating a dev database with seed data.

## What You Must Build

### 1. `api/internal/testutil/testdb.go` — Shared test helper

```go
package testutil

import (
    "context"
    "testing"
    "github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
)

// TestDB creates a PostgreSQL container with pgvector, runs migrations,
// and returns a connection pool. The container is cleaned up when the
// test completes.
func TestDB(t *testing.T) *pgxpool.Pool

// TestDBWithSeed creates a test database and populates it with seed data.
func TestDBWithSeed(t *testing.T) *pgxpool.Pool
```

Implementation details:
- Use `testcontainers-go` with the PostgreSQL module
- Use the `pgvector/pgvector:pg16` Docker image (has pgvector pre-installed)
- After container starts, run migrations using `db.MigrateUp(ctx, databaseURL)`
- Use `t.Cleanup()` for container teardown
- Cache the container per test package using `sync.Once` if possible (for speed)
- Skip tests if Docker is not available: `if testing.Short() { t.Skip("skipping: requires Docker") }`

### 2. `api/internal/testutil/seed.go` — Realistic seed data

Create functions that insert realistic sample data:

```go
// SeedAll inserts all seed data into the database.
func SeedAll(ctx context.Context, pool *pgxpool.Pool) error

// SeedUsers creates sample users and returns their IDs.
func SeedUsers(ctx context.Context, pool *pgxpool.Pool) ([]uuid.UUID, error)

// SeedItems creates sample items of various types for the given owner.
func SeedItems(ctx context.Context, pool *pgxpool.Pool, ownerID uuid.UUID) error
```

Sample data should be realistic and cover all item types:

**Users** (2-3):
- "Ketan Gangatirkar" (ketan@example.com) — primary test user
- "Test User" (test@example.com) — secondary user for multi-tenant testing
- "Admin User" (admin@example.com) — admin role

**Bookmarks** (3-4 per user):
- A Hacker News article about distributed systems
- A blog post about Go performance optimization
- A Wikipedia article about knowledge management
- A GitHub repository README

**Voice Notes** (2 per user):
- A meeting recap about project planning (with realistic transcription text)
- A quick thought about a book recommendation

**Text Notes** (2-3 per user):
- A recipe with ingredients list
- A travel packing list
- Personal journal entry about career goals

**MCP Clients** (2):
- "Claude Desktop" with search/browse permissions
- "Cursor" with search-only permissions

**Audit Log Entries** (5-6):
- Mix of search, read, create actions from different clients

For each item, include realistic:
- `tags` (hierarchical: "project/flotsam", "topic/golang", "person/sarah")
- `metadata` JSONB (type-specific fields from the spec)
- `auto_metadata` JSONB (category, topics, sentiment)
- `source` (chrome_extension, android_app, web_app)
- Varied `sensitivity` levels
- Varied `status` (mostly 'ready', some 'pending')
- Realistic timestamps spread over the past 30 days

Password hashes should use bcrypt (import `golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt`).

### 3. `api/cmd/seed/main.go` — Seed data CLI tool

A small binary that connects to the database and runs the seed data:
```go
func main() {
    // Load .env, parse DATABASE_URL, connect, run SeedAll
}
```

### 4. Makefile target

Add to `api/Makefile`:
```makefile
## seed: Populate database with sample data
seed:
	go run ./cmd/seed
```

And to root `Makefile`:
```makefile
## api-seed: Populate dev database with sample data
api-seed:
	$(MAKE) -C api seed
```

### 5. Tests

**`api/internal/testutil/testdb_test.go`**:
- Test that TestDB creates a working connection (integration test, needs Docker)
- Test that TestDBWithSeed populates all expected tables
- Verify row counts after seeding

### 6. Update existing `api/internal/db/db_test.go`

The current integration tests in db_test.go manually check for `DATABASE_URL`. Update them to optionally use testcontainers when `DATABASE_URL` is not set but Docker is available. This makes integration tests more portable.

## Existing Code Context

**`api/internal/db/db.go`** — has `Connect(ctx, databaseURL)` and `MigrateUp(ctx, databaseURL)` / `MigrateDown(ctx, databaseURL)`

**`api/migrations/00001_initial_schema.sql`** — full schema with users, items, mcp_clients, audit_log tables

**Database enums**: item_type ('bookmark','voice_note','text_note','drive_doc','highlight','file'), item_status ('pending','processing','ready','error'), sensitivity_level ('public','normal','sensitive','private')

## Quality Gate
```bash
make api-test-unit && make api-lint
```
Note: The testcontainer integration tests will be skipped in unit test mode (-short). That's fine — they'll run in `make api-test`.

## Conventions
- Error wrapping: `fmt.Errorf("testutil: context: %w", err)`
- Use `t.Helper()` in test helper functions
- Context: always first parameter

## Commit Convention
```
Add testcontainer DB helper and realistic seed data

Provides testutil.TestDB() for spinning up pgvector containers in
integration tests. Includes seed data covering all item types with
realistic content, tags, and metadata.

Issues: flt-it8.19
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

## WORKTREE ISOLATION — CRITICAL

### Create your worktree (MANDATORY FIRST STEP)
```bash
WORKTREE_NAME="testcontainer"
REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$WORKTREE_NAME"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/$WORKTREE_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi
cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```

Then verify:
```bash
echo "=== WORKTREE VERIFICATION ===" && \
echo "CWD: $(pwd)" && \
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)" && \
if echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/'; then \
  echo "✓ WORKTREE VERIFIED"; \
else \
  echo "✗ FATAL: NOT in a worktree! CWD=$(pwd)"; exit 1; \
fi
```

### Rules
1. NEVER cd to the main repo — stay in your worktree
2. Before EVERY git commit, re-verify CWD is in `.claude/worktrees/`
3. Push with `git push origin HEAD`

## Context
Flotsam needs an S3-compatible storage abstraction for uploading bookmark HTML, voice note audio, and generic files. Config fields and .env.example already exist. The `aws-sdk-go-v2` SDK needs to be added to go.mod.

## Files to Create

### 1. `api/internal/storage/storage.go`
- `Config` struct (Endpoint, Bucket, AccessKey, SecretKey, Region, ForcePathStyle)
- `Client` struct wrapping `*s3.Client` + `*s3.PresignClient` + bucket string
- `New(cfg Config) (*Client, error)` — builds AWS config with custom endpoint resolver, static credentials, path-style option
- `Upload(ctx, key, body io.Reader, contentType string) error` — `PutObject`
- `Download(ctx, key) (io.ReadCloser, error)` — `GetObject`
- `Delete(ctx, key) error` — `DeleteObject`
- `Exists(ctx, key) (bool, error)` — `HeadObject`, return false on `NotFound`
- `PresignedURL(ctx, key, expiry time.Duration) (string, error)` — presign `GetObject`

### 2. `api/internal/storage/keys.go`
- `KeyFor(ownerID uuid.UUID, itemType string, itemID uuid.UUID, filename string) string`
- `BookmarkKey(ownerID, itemID uuid.UUID) string` → `{owner_id}/bookmarks/{item_id}/content.html`
- `AudioKey(ownerID, itemID uuid.UUID, format string) string` → `{owner_id}/voice_notes/{item_id}/audio.{format}`
- `FileKey(ownerID, itemID uuid.UUID, filename string) string` → `{owner_id}/files/{item_id}/{filename}`

### 3. `api/internal/storage/keys_test.go`
